fail-fast-guard-clauses.notes: validateOrder uses early-return guard clauses. handleUrgentOrder/handleNormalOrder check validation first and return early on error.

max-2-indentation.notes: Maximum indentation is 2 levels (if inside async function). Clean flat structure throughout.

switch-over-if-else.notes: No if-else chains that warrant a switch. Sequential guard clauses are appropriate here.

no-boolean-flags.notes: Two separate named functions exist: handleUrgentOrder and handleNormalOrder. No boolean dispatch parameter.

declarative-over-imperative.notes: No mutation of input objects. Order.items is readonly. Results are returned as new objects.

parallelize-async.notes: Promise.all used for processOrder + fetchInventory + fetchPricing in both handlers.

minor_observations: handleUrgentOrder and handleNormalOrder are nearly identical -- only the process function differs. A shared helper could reduce duplication, but this is a style concern, not a rule violation.
